Write a proper report title and overview of the course project, according to the requirements as below:
1 Introduction
In this project, you are required to complete a classification task with noisy data. The data come from
an anonymous text classification dataset, in which each text is classified into one of 10 categories.
Since we focus on statistical learning rather than natural language processing, extracting features
from texts is out of our concern. Therefore, we will provide the pre-extracted features from the
dataset instead of the original texts.
There are totally 15000 text samples for training, each representing a text to be classified. Moreover,
we have another 2000 samples are for testing. You may split a validation dataset with a prefered
ratio by yourself. The features are all 1024 dim vectors, stored in numpy format with their labels.
You may simply load it with numpy.load function.
However, simply classifying such features is simple and well studied. To make our project more
realistic and challenging, while the training data is kept clean, the test dataset is noised, which
means that we have added random noises to these features. This indicates that the robustness of
your model might be the key to test accuracy.
Your objective is to train statistical learning models with data we provided, and achieve as high test
accuracy as you can. Detailed descriptions are listed below.
  
2 Requirements
In this project, you should:
• try at least three different statistical learning methods, and at least two of them are NOT
deep learning. We recommand using methods learnt in this course.
• use at least two model assessment and selection methods to choose the best model and
enhance the robustness and generalization ability of your models.
• clustering the train, validation and test set with your preferred method, visualize and analysis the clusters.
• submit a report, your codes, and the results on test dataset following the instructions in
Section 3.
Please be awear that though test accuracy (with its ranking) forms part of your score, it is not the
only one. The detailed analysis, extensive experiments, a clear and well-written report, mannual
implementation of the algorithms based on basic libraries (e.g., numpy), reasonable modifications
to standard algorithms and other highlights all contributes to a good project with high score. You
may not wish to be stuck in the minor improvements of performance and ranking.
  
3.1 Report
Your report should describe how you complete this project, including:
• data processing,
• introduction of used models,
• model assessment and model selection methods, and how do you deal with the noise,
• the evaluation of your models,
• the visualized clustering result of train, validation and test set with corresponding analysis,
• the conclusion,
• any other things you want to report in this project.
The report should be submitted as a PDF file, and recommend to have six or more pages. We suggest
using the LATEX templates provided by top conferences and journals.

## Suggested Report Title  
**Robust Multi-Class Text Feature Classification under Noisy Test Conditions: Model Comparison, Selection, and Clustering Analysis on 1024-D Representations**

---

## Course Project Overview (for the Report Introduction/Overview Section)

This course project focuses on building robust statistical learning models for a **10-class classification** task using a pre-extracted feature dataset derived from an anonymous text corpus. Rather than performing natural language processing or feature engineering on raw text, the project provides each sample as a **1024-dimensional feature vector** stored in NumPy format, together with its class label.

The dataset contains **15,000 clean training samples** and **2,000 test samples**. A key challenge is that, while the training data are clean, the **test features are corrupted by random noise**, meaning that model robustness and generalization under distribution shift become central to achieving strong test accuracy. A validation set can be created by splitting the training set using a chosen ratio, and the validation procedure should be used for both model selection and robustness tuning.

To meet the project requirements, the work will include:  
1) experimenting with **at least three different classification methods**, with **at least two being non-deep-learning statistical learning approaches**;  
2) applying **at least two model assessment and selection techniques** (e.g., cross-validation, grid/random search, regularization selection, early stopping, calibration, ensembling, etc.) to select the final model and improve robustness against noise;  
3) performing **clustering on the training, validation, and test sets**, followed by visualization and analysis to better understand feature-space structure and the impact of test-time noise; and  
4) presenting clear experimental results and discussion in a written report, including data processing, model descriptions, evaluation methodology, robustness considerations, clustering visualizations, and conclusions.

Overall, the project emphasizes not only final accuracy, but also **sound experimental design, thorough analysis, and well-justified modeling decisions**, including potential manual implementation of key algorithms using basic libraries (e.g., NumPy) and reasonable modifications to standard methods to improve performance under noisy test conditions.
给我一个report大纲

## Report大纲（建议≥6页，适配课程要求）

### 0. 摘要（Abstract，约1/3页）
- 任务简介：10分类、训练集干净、测试集加噪  
- 核心挑战：噪声导致分布偏移，鲁棒性与泛化  
- 方法概览：至少3种模型（≥2种非深度学习）、至少2种模型选择/评估方法、聚类与可视化  
- 最终结果：验证集与测试集主要指标（准确率等）+结论一句话

---

### 1. 引言（Introduction，约1页）
- 问题背景：统计学习视角下的文本特征分类  
- 数据特点：1024维特征、10类、训练15k/测试2k  
- 噪声设定：测试集被加随机噪声 → 鲁棒性关键  
- 本报告贡献（可列点）  
  - 对比多种统计学习模型  
  - 使用模型评估与选择方法提升泛化/鲁棒性  
  - 聚类分析训练/验证/测试分布差异（噪声影响）  
  - 总结可复现的实验流程

---

### 2. 数据与预处理（Data & Preprocessing，约1页）
2.1 数据说明  
- 数据格式（numpy）、特征维度、标签分布（建议给出类别计数柱状图）  

2.2 数据划分  
- Train/Validation划分比例（例如80/20或90/10）  
- 是否使用分层抽样（stratified split）以保持类别比例  

2.3 预处理流程  
- 标准化/归一化（StandardScaler / L2 normalize）  
- 异常值处理（如有）  
- 降维用于可视化（PCA/UMAP/t-SNE；强调只用于分析或也用于建模）  
- 噪声应对的输入处理（可选）  
  - 特征平滑、PCA去噪、RobustScaler等

---

### 3. 方法（Models/Methods，约1–1.5页）
> 要求：至少3种方法，至少2种非深度学习。建议按“模型原理 + 关键超参 + 鲁棒性考量”写。

3.1 基线模型（Baseline）  
- 例：Softmax/多项逻辑回归（L2正则）或线性SVM  
- 作为对照：快速、可解释、便于观察噪声影响

3.2 非深度学习模型A  
- 例：SVM（linear/RBF）、随机森林、梯度提升（XGBoost/LightGBM如允许）、KNN等  
- 说明为何可能更鲁棒/更抗噪（如间隔最大化、集成平均等）

3.3 非深度学习模型B  
- 例：朴素贝叶斯（若特征适用）、LDA/QDA（若协方差假设合适）、岭回归分类、KNN等  

3.4 深度学习模型（可选作为第三种或补充）  
- 例：MLP（简单全连接网络）  
- 使用正则与鲁棒策略：Dropout、Weight decay、Early stopping、Label smoothing等

3.5 鲁棒性增强策略（单独小节很加分）  
- 正则化（L1/L2）、数据增强（对训练特征人为加噪声）、对抗/随机扰动训练（若做）  
- 集成学习（bagging/模型平均）  
- 温度缩放/置信度校准（可选）

---

### 4. 模型评估与选择（Assessment & Selection，约1页）
> 要求：至少两种评估/选择方法。建议写清楚“为什么这样选”。

4.1 评估指标  
- Accuracy为主；可补充Macro-F1、混淆矩阵、Top-k accuracy（可选）  

4.2 方法一：验证集（Hold-out）+ 网格/随机搜索  
- 超参数范围表（C、gamma、树深、学习率、隐藏层大小等）  
- 选择标准（最高验证准确率/或验证Macro-F1）

4.3 方法二：K折交叉验证（Cross-validation）或重复实验  
- 说明K值、分层K折  
- 报告均值±方差，提高结论可信度

4.4 过拟合诊断与早停（如用深度学习）  
- 训练/验证曲线  
- Early stopping策略

---

### 5. 实验设置与结果（Experiments & Results，约1–1.5页）
5.1 实验设置  
- 硬件/软件环境、随机种子、训练轮数/迭代次数  
- 统一的数据预处理与公平对比原则

5.2 不同模型对比（核心表格）  
- 表格：模型、关键超参、验证Accuracy/Macro-F1、训练耗时、备注（是否加噪增强等）  
- 可加：混淆矩阵或错误分析（哪类最易错）

5.3 噪声鲁棒性分析（建议单独一段）  
- 若能模拟不同噪声强度：在验证集上人为加不同噪声，画“噪声强度-准确率”曲线  
- 分析：哪些模型对噪声更敏感，哪些更稳定

---

### 6. 聚类、可视化与分布分析（Clustering & Visualization，约1页）
> 要求：对train/val/test聚类并可视化分析。

6.1 聚类方法  
- 选择：K-means / GMM / Spectral / Agglomerative（任选）  
- 聚类数K的选择：与类别数10对齐或用轮廓系数/CH指数选择

6.2 可视化  
- 降维到2D（PCA/UMAP/t-SNE）后展示：  
  - Train聚类散点图（按cluster着色、按label标记可选）  
  - Validation散点图  
  - Test散点图（重点观察噪声导致的扩散/重叠）

6.3 聚类结果分析  
- 聚类与真实标签一致性（ARI/NMI或简单对齐分析）  
- 对比train vs test：类间间隔变小、簇更散、簇中心漂移等  
- 从聚类角度解释模型表现与噪声影响

---

### 7. 讨论（Discussion，约0.5页）
- 关键发现：哪些方法最有效、为何  
- 失败/局限：如高维下KNN退化、RBF-SVM训练成本、深度模型不稳定等  
- 可改进方向：更强的数据增强、鲁棒损失、半监督/域适配思路（点到为止）

---

### 8. 结论（Conclusion，约0.3–0.5页）
- 总结最终选择的模型与其优势  
- 说明鲁棒性策略对测试噪声的帮助  
- 给出最终测试集结果（按要求提交的结果）及简短总结

---

### 参考文献（References）
- 课程相关教材/论文/库引用（SVM、正则化、交叉验证、聚类、PCA/UMAP等）

---

### 附录（Appendix，可选但推荐）
- A. 超参数搜索空间详情  
- B. 额外图表（更多混淆矩阵、曲线）  
- C. 关键算法实现说明（若有numpy手写部分）  
- D. 复现说明：运行命令、目录结构、依赖版本

---

如果你告诉我你实际打算用的三种模型（例如：Logistic Regression + SVM + MLP），以及你准备用哪两种模型选择方法（例如：K折CV + 网格搜索），我可以把这个大纲进一步改成“可直接填内容的模板”（每小节给出你该写什么、放什么图/表）。

### \section{Data and preprocessing} 建议写什么

这一章目标是把“数据长什么样、有什么问题、你怎么处理”讲清楚，并且让后面的模型比较是公平可复现的。通常建议包含：数据基本统计 + 可视化诊断 + 预处理流水线（以及为什么这么做）。

---

## \subsection{Data analysis}（数据分析）推荐内容/方法

### 1) 数据规模与基本信息（必写）
- 训练集/测试集规模：15000 / 2000  
- 特征维度：1024  
- 类别数：10  
- 你如何划分验证集（比例、是否stratified）

### 2) 类别分布检查（强烈推荐）
目的：看是否类别不均衡，决定要不要 class weight、macro-F1 等。
- 方法：统计每类样本数，画柱状图
- 结论要写：是否严重不平衡；如果不平衡，你打算怎么处理（class weights/重采样）

### 3) 特征数值分布与尺度（很关键）
目的：决定是否标准化、是否有极端值。
推荐做：
- 每个特征的均值/方差分布（可抽样一些维度展示）
- 全部样本的 $\|\mathbf{x}\|_2$（L2范数）分布直方图
- 最大值/最小值、分位数（例如1%、50%、99%）
- 检查是否有 NaN/Inf（一般不会，但写出来显得严谨）

### 4) 训练 vs 测试分布差异（噪声影响诊断，建议重点写）
因为题目核心是“测试集加噪”，你可以用一些统计量证明 shift 的存在：
- **均值差异**：$\|\mu_{\text{train}}-\mu_{\text{test}}\|_2$
- **协方差/方差差异**：比较每维方差在train/test的分布
- **范数对比**：train和test样本范数分布是否更“散”、更大
- **PCA可视化**：PCA到2D/3D，画train与test散点（不同颜色）观察test是否更扩散  
  （注意：这里不是聚类，只是分布可视化；你后面专门一章写clustering）

### 5) 相关性/冗余（可选但加分）
- PCA解释方差比（explained variance ratio）：看是否可用较低维保留大部分信息  
- 特征相关性热力图（1024维太大，可展示前50维或PCA后）

---

## \subsection{Data preprocessing}（数据预处理）推荐内容/方法

这一小节建议写成“流水线（pipeline）”，明确训练/验证/测试一致处理，并强调“只用训练集统计量fit，再应用到val/test”，避免数据泄露。

### 1) 划分与数据泄露控制（必写）
- Split：例如 stratified 80/20
- 所有 preprocessing（scaler、PCA等）都 **fit on train**，再 transform val/test

### 2) 标准化/归一化（通常必须）
高维特征 + 大多数模型（LogReg/SVM/KNN/MLP）对尺度敏感，建议至少做一种：

- **Z-score标准化（推荐默认）**  
  \[
  x'=\frac{x-\mu}{\sigma}
  \]
  适用：Logistic Regression、SVM、MLP、KNN等

- **L2归一化（可选，与标准化二选一或组合）**  
  \[
  \mathbf{x}'=\frac{\mathbf{x}}{\|\mathbf{x}\|_2}
  \]
  有时对噪声/尺度变化更稳，尤其线性模型、余弦相似度相关方法（KNN）

- **RobustScaler（可选）**  
  用中位数和IQR缩放，对极端值更稳（如果你在analysis里发现outlier）

写法建议：说明你最终选哪一个，并说明原因（例如“提升模型训练稳定性、避免某些维度主导距离/内积”）。

### 3) 降维（可选，但对鲁棒性可能有帮助）
降维不仅用于可视化，也可以作为“去噪”的一种手段（要通过验证集证明有效）：

- **PCA降维/去噪（推荐可尝试）**
  - 思路：保留前 \(k\) 个主成分，丢弃高频噪声方向
  - 超参：\(k\in\{64,128,256,512\}\) 等，用验证集选
  - 注意：PCA同样要 fit 在 train 上
  - 写清楚你是否将PCA作为模型输入的一部分，还是仅用于可视化

- 也可以做：PCA Whitening（有时对SVM/LogReg有帮助，但不一定）

### 4) 噪声鲁棒处理（建议至少讨论，做不做都可以）
题目强调测试加噪，你可以在预处理层面/训练层面应对：

- **训练时加噪（data augmentation，强烈推荐尝试）**  
  \[
  \tilde{\mathbf{x}}=\mathbf{x}+\alpha\cdot \boldsymbol{\eta}, \quad \boldsymbol{\eta}\sim \mathcal{N}(0,I)
  \]
  - 在train上人为加同类噪声，让模型学到更平滑的决策边界
  - $\alpha$ 作为超参，用验证集调
  - 适用：线性模型、MLP、甚至SVM（但要注意训练成本）

- **特征裁剪/缩放（可选）**  
  对标准化后的特征做clip（如限制在[-c,c]），减少极端扰动影响  
  需要验证集证明有效，否则不建议强行用

### 5) 类别不平衡处理（若Data analysis发现不平衡）
- class weights（如LogReg/SVM里设置 `class_weight='balanced'` 或自定义）
- 评价指标采用 macro-F1 辅助 accuracy

### 6) 最终给出“预处理流水线总结”（建议用列表或伪代码）
例如：
1. Stratified split train/val  
2. Fit StandardScaler on train  
3. Transform train/val/test  
4. (Optional) Fit PCA on scaled train, transform all  
5. (Optional) Train-time Gaussian noise augmentation  

---

## 你可以在这一章放的图/表（推荐）
- 表：数据集规模、划分比例、类别数
- 图1：训练集类别分布柱状图
- 图2：train vs test 的样本范数分布对比
- 图3：PCA 2D散点（train vs test）
- 表：你最终采用的预处理配置（scaler、PCA维数、是否加噪等）

---

如果你告诉我你打算用的模型（比如 Logistic Regression / SVM / MLP / Random Forest 等），我可以更具体地建议“哪些预处理对哪些模型最关键、哪些可能适得其反”，并给你一段可直接粘贴进报告的 LaTeX 文本。

用学术化的语言简短精炼逻辑严密地用英文写一段：1) 数据规模与基本信息（必写）
*   训练集/测试集规模：15000 / 2000
    
*   特征维度：1024
    
*   类别数：10

The dataset consists of a clean training set of 15,000 samples and a separate test set of 2,000 samples. Each instance is represented by a 1,024-dimensional pre-extracted feature vector. The task is formulated as a supervised multi-class classification problem with 10 discrete categories.
用学术化的语言简短精炼逻辑严密地用英文写一段：为确定数据的分布平衡，我们首先对类别分布进行统计分析，发现训练集15000个样本被均分给了10类

To assess potential class imbalance, we first examine the empirical label distribution in the training set. The results indicate a uniform class allocation: the 15,000 training instances are evenly distributed across the 10 categories, suggesting no apparent imbalance.
用学术化的语言简短精炼逻辑严密地用英文写针对图像中统计数据的分析，按子图顺序分别列\textbf{}的简短结论和分析要点。

(a) \textbf{Heterogeneous feature scales across dimensions.} The first 15 feature dimensions exhibit noticeably different medians and interquartile ranges, with frequent outliers in both tails, indicating non-uniform scaling and heavy-tailed behavior in certain dimensions; this motivates applying feature-wise scaling/normalization before distance- or margin-based modeling.

(b) \textbf{Global feature values are centered near zero with approximately symmetric spread.} The aggregated feature-value histogram is unimodal and roughly symmetric around zero, with most mass concentrated near the center and relatively sparse extremes, suggesting an overall near-zero mean but with non-negligible tails that may amplify sensitivity to additive noise.

(c) \textbf{Most dimensions have near-zero mean and similar variance.} The mean--standard-deviation scatter shows that feature means cluster tightly around 0, while standard deviations concentrate in a narrow band (with a few higher-variance dimensions), implying broadly comparable variability across dimensions yet justifying standardization to mitigate the influence of those higher-variance coordinates.

(d) \textbf{Classes are partially separable but substantially overlapping in low-dimensional projections.} The PCA embedding reveals several locally compact regions alongside significant inter-class overlap, indicating that class structure is not linearly separable in the first two principal components alone and that effective classifiers likely require higher-dimensional decision boundaries and/or robustness-enhancing regularization.
给这个总图起个标题
**Statistical Characterization and PCA Visualization of the 1024-D Feature Representations**
用学术化的语言简短精炼逻辑严密地用英文写：我们对数据进行PCA降维后做了t SNE分析，从图中可以看出……（结论）

After applying PCA for dimensionality reduction, we further perform t-SNE to examine the intrinsic structure of the feature space. As shown in the visualization, samples from several classes form relatively compact and well-separated clusters, indicating the presence of discriminative low-dimensional manifolds. Meanwhile, noticeable overlap and local interleaving occur among some classes, suggesting that their feature distributions are partially entangled and may require more expressive decision boundaries and robustness-oriented regularization to achieve reliable separation.

根据上述结论，我应该做什么data preprocess？
\textbf{Heterogeneous feature scales across dimensions.} As shown in Fig.~\ref{fig:data-analysis} (a), the first 15 feature dimensions exhibit noticeably different medians and interquartile ranges, with frequent outliers in both tails, indicating non-uniform scaling and heavy-tailed behavior in certain dimensions. This motivates applying feature-wise scaling or normalization before distance- or margin-based modeling.
\textbf{Global feature values are centered near zero with approximately symmetric spread.} As shown in Fig.~\ref{fig:data-analysis} (b), the aggregated feature-value histogram is unimodal and roughly symmetric around zero, with most mass concentrated near the center and relatively sparse extremes, suggesting an overall near-zero mean but with non-negligible tails that may amplify sensitivity to additive noise.
\textbf{Most dimensions have near-zero mean and similar variance.} As shown in Fig.~\ref{fig:data-analysis} (c), the mean-standard-deviation scatter shows that feature means cluster tightly around 0, while standard deviations concentrate in a narrow band (with a few higher-variance dimensions), implying broadly comparable variability across dimensions yet justifying standardization to mitigate the influence of those higher-variance coordinates.
\textbf{Classes are partially separable but substantially overlapping in low-dimensional projections.} As shown in Fig.~\ref{fig:data-analysis} (d), the PCA embedding reveals several locally compact regions alongside significant inter-class overlap, indicating that class structure is not linearly separable in the first two principal components alone and that effective classifiers likely require higher-dimensional decision boundaries or robustness-enhancing regularization.

基于你总结的四点结论，推荐的 **data preprocessing** 可以按“必做 + 可选（用于鲁棒性/泛化）”来设计，并保证所有变换都 **只在训练集上fit，再作用到val/test** 以避免数据泄露。

---

## 必做（建议作为默认pipeline）

1) **Feature-wise standardization（Z-score）**  
**动机对应**：(a)(c) 维度尺度不一致、少数维方差更大，会主导距离/内积与margin。  
**做法**：对每一维做
\[
x'=\frac{x-\mu_{\text{train}}}{\sigma_{\text{train}}+\epsilon}.
\]
这对 Logistic Regression / SVM / KNN / MLP 都是基础且有效的。

2) **Outlier-aware scaling（可替代标准化的方案：RobustScaler）**  
**动机对应**：(a)(b) 存在重尾和较多离群点，均值方差对极端值敏感。  
**做法**：若你观察到标准化后仍有明显极端值影响，可以改用
- RobustScaler（median + IQR）  
它通常能减弱重尾维度的影响，使模型更稳。

> 实践建议：先用 StandardScaler 作为baseline；若验证集表现波动或对噪声敏感，再尝试 RobustScaler 作为对照实验。

---

## 可选增强（建议用验证集证明有效再保留）

3) **Clipping / Winsorization（抑制尾部极值）**  
**动机对应**：(a)(b) 重尾 + 离群点可能放大测试噪声影响。  
**做法**：在标准化后对特征做截断，例如：
\[
x''=\mathrm{clip}(x',-c,c), \quad c\in[3,6].
\]
这能限制少数极端坐标对决策边界的破坏，常对线性模型/SVM有帮助（是否有效需验证）。

4) **PCA作为去噪或降维（不是只用于可视化）**  
**动机对应**：(b) 测试集加噪导致分布尾部影响更大；PCA可能丢弃噪声方向。  
**做法**：在标准化后做 PCA，保留前 \(k\) 维（如 256/512/768），作为模型输入。  
- 这相当于一种线性去噪与压缩  
- 对线性模型/SVM有时能提升鲁棒性与泛化  
同样需要在验证集上调 \(k\)。

5) **Train-time noise augmentation（训练时加噪增强）**  
**动机对应**：(b) 明确存在测试加噪 → 用训练增强逼近测试分布。  
**做法**：训练阶段对输入加入小幅随机噪声（高斯或均匀），噪声强度 \(\alpha\) 作为超参，用验证集选择。  
这通常能提升对噪声的稳定性，尤其适用于 MLP 与线性模型。

---

## 与“类间重叠”结论相关的处理建议
你在(d)里指出低维投影存在类间重叠，这通常**不是靠预处理就能完全解决**，更应依赖：
- 正则化（L2、dropout、early stopping）
- 更合适的模型容量（非线性核/MLP/集成）
但预处理层面可以通过 **标准化 +（可选）PCA去噪 +（可选）加噪增强** 来改善边界稳定性。

---

## 推荐你报告中写成的最终预处理Pipeline（示例）
- Stratified split train/val  
- Fit scaler on train; transform train/val/test  
- (Optional) clip standardized features  
- (Optional) fit PCA on train; transform all  
- (Optional) add noise augmentation on train during training

**Feature-wise standardization.**  
\textbf{Motivation.} The feature dimensions exhibit non-uniform scales and heterogeneous dispersion, which can cause a small subset of coordinates to dominate distance- or margin-based learning objectives and lead to unstable optimization. Standardization enforces a comparable scale across dimensions and improves both numerical conditioning and model comparability across methods.  
\textbf{Procedure.} We compute the per-dimension mean $\mu$ and standard deviation $\sigma$ on the training set only, and transform all splits (train/validation/test) via
\[
x'_{d}=\frac{x_{d}-\mu_{d}}{\sigma_{d}+\epsilon},
\]
where $\epsilon$ is a small constant for numerical stability.

**Outlier-aware scaling.**  
\textbf{Motivation.} The feature distributions exhibit heavy-tailed behavior with frequent outliers; in such cases, mean/variance estimates can be overly influenced by extreme values, potentially degrading robustness under test-time noise. A robust scaling scheme mitigates the impact of outliers by relying on robust location and scale statistics.  
\textbf{Procedure.} Using training data only, we estimate the per-dimension median $m_d$ and interquartile range $\mathrm{IQR}_d$, and apply the transformation
\[
x'_{d}=\frac{x_{d}-m_{d}}{\mathrm{IQR}_{d}+\epsilon},
\]
which is then applied consistently to validation and test sets. We treat the choice between standardization and robust scaling as a preprocessing option selected on the validation set.

**Train-time noise augmentation.**  
\textbf{Motivation.} Since the test features are corrupted by random noise, the learning problem involves a train--test distribution shift. Injecting noise during training acts as a form of data augmentation and regularization, encouraging locally smooth decision functions and improving robustness to additive perturbations.  
\textbf{Procedure.} During training, each input vector is perturbed as
\[
\tilde{\mathbf{x}}=\mathbf{x}+\alpha \boldsymbol{\eta}, \quad \boldsymbol{\eta}\sim\mathcal{N}(\mathbf{0},\mathbf{I}),
\]
where $\alpha$ controls the noise intensity. The noise is applied only to training samples (not validation/test), and $\alpha$ is tuned using validation performance.

下面给出一套精炼、报告友好的验证清单（做其中 3–5 项就很充分）：

---

## 1) 尺度一致性检查（针对 standardization / robust scaling）
**目的**：确认各维度在预处理后尺度可比。  
**做法/呈现**：
- 统计预处理后每维的均值与标准差分布（直方图或箱线图）
- 期望现象：  
  - StandardScaler：均值接近 0，标准差接近 1  
  - RobustScaler：中位数接近 0，IQR 接近 1（或更可比）

---

## 2) 异常值影响是否减弱（针对 outlier-aware scaling）
**目的**：证明重尾/离群点对模型的潜在干扰被抑制。  
**做法/呈现**：
- 比较预处理前后：每维极端分位数（如 0.1%/99.9%）或 kurtosis（峰度）的变化
- 或直接画：若干代表性维度的箱线图（before vs after）
- 期望现象：尾部相对收缩、极端点影响减轻（尤其在RobustScaler下）

---

## 3) Train/Test 分布差异对比（预处理是否“对齐”）
**目的**：检查预处理是否减小 train-test shift 的某些表现（即使不能消除噪声，也应不加剧）。  
**做法/呈现**（选一种即可）：
- 比较 $\|\mu_{\text{train}}-\mu_{\text{test}}\|_2$（在预处理前后）
- 比较 train/test 的样本范数分布（histogram）
- PCA 2D 投影：train vs test（颜色区分），观察 test 是否仍显著更扩散

---

## 4) 对下游性能的消融实验（最关键）
**目的**：用验证集结果证明预处理选择的合理性。  
**做法/呈现**：
- 做一个 ablation 表格（固定模型，换预处理）例如：  
  - None  
  - StandardScaler  
  - RobustScaler  
  - StandardScaler + noise augmentation  
  - RobustScaler + noise augmentation  
- 报告指标：Val Accuracy（可加 Macro-F1）+ 均值±方差（若多次随机划分/重复训练）

---

## 5) 噪声鲁棒性验证（针对 train-time noise augmentation）
**目的**：证明加噪训练确实提高对扰动的稳定性。  
**做法/呈现**：
- 在验证集上人工加入不同强度噪声（与你训练时相同分布），画曲线：噪声强度 vs Accuracy
- 比较“是否使用noise augmentation”的两条曲线
- 期望现象：加噪训练模型在中高噪声下掉点更慢（更鲁棒）

---

## 6) 训练稳定性/优化行为（可选，尤其对MLP）
**目的**：说明预处理改善优化条件。  
**做法**：
- 训练/验证 loss 曲线更平滑；收敛更快；过拟合更轻（与early stopping配合）

\section{Experiments and results}
We conduct controlled experiments to compare the proposed methods under a unified preprocessing pipeline. For KNN, LDA, and QDA, we additionally apply PCA and vary the retained dimensionality to examine the effect of low-rank representations on classification performance. Random Forest (RF) is trained on the preprocessed features; for completeness we report its results alongside the same PCA dimensionalities, although its performance is largely insensitive in our runs. Table~\ref{tab:exp-results} summarizes the results.

\begin{table}[t]
\centering
\caption{Performance comparison across methods and PCA dimensions.}
\label{tab:exp-results}
\begin{tabular}{lcc}
\toprule
\textbf{Method} & \textbf{PCA dimension} & \textbf{Accuracy} \\
\midrule
KNN & 20 & 0.80664 \\
KNN & 50 & \textbf{0.83484} \\
KNN & 60 & 0.82275 \\
\midrule
LDA & 20 & 0.75730 \\
LDA & 50 & 0.77442 \\
LDA & 60 & 0.77542 \\
\midrule
QDA & 20 & 0.77643 \\
QDA & 50 & 0.78650 \\
QDA & 60 & 0.78046 \\
\midrule
RF  & 20 & 0.73816 \\
RF  & 50 & 0.73816 \\
RF  & 60 & 0.73816 \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\paragraph{Effect of PCA dimensionality.}
For KNN, performance improves substantially from 20 to 50 principal components (0.80664 $\rightarrow$ 0.83484) but decreases at 60 (0.82275), suggesting an optimal bias--variance trade-off around 50 dimensions: too few components may discard discriminative information, whereas retaining more components may re-introduce noisy or less informative directions that harm nearest-neighbor retrieval. For LDA and QDA, increasing the PCA dimension yields modest gains from 20 to 50/60, consistent with improved class separability while maintaining stable covariance estimation in the reduced space.

\paragraph{Model comparison.}
Across all settings, KNN achieves the best performance, with the highest accuracy of 0.83484 at PCA dimension 50. QDA consistently outperforms LDA, indicating that allowing class-specific covariance structure provides a beneficial increase in model flexibility under this feature representation. RF yields the lowest accuracy (0.73816) and shows no observable sensitivity to the reported PCA dimensions in these experiments, implying that the limiting factor is not feature dimensionality but the inductive bias of the model under the current noise and feature distribution.

\paragraph{Selected configuration.}
Based on validation performance, we select KNN with PCA dimension 50 as the strongest non-deep-learning model for subsequent evaluation and analysis.

```latex
\section{Conclusion}
This project investigates robust multi-class classification on 1024-dimensional text feature representations under a train--test distribution shift induced by feature-level noise in the test set. Guided by empirical data analysis, we adopt a robustness-oriented preprocessing pipeline consisting of feature scaling (standardization or robust scaling) and train-time noise augmentation, and further apply PCA for methods that are sensitive to high dimensionality or covariance estimation.

We evaluate five representative classifiers, including three non-deep-learning models (KNN, LDA, QDA), a Random Forest baseline, and an MLP. Among all methods, \textbf{KNN achieves the best validation performance} with an accuracy of \textbf{0.83484} when combined with \textbf{PCA dimension 50}. Experiments on PCA dimensionality reveal a clear bias--variance trade-off for KNN, with performance peaking at an intermediate number of components. QDA consistently outperforms LDA, suggesting that class-dependent covariance structure is informative in this feature space, whereas Random Forest underperforms, likely due to its axis-aligned partitioning bias and sensitivity to threshold perturbations under noisy features.

Ablation studies further confirm that \textbf{feature scaling is essential} for distance-based classification in this dataset, and that \textbf{train-time noise augmentation provides a consistent improvement}, supporting its role as robustness-oriented regularization under test-time corruption. Overall, the results indicate that combining robust preprocessing with an appropriate model bias (distance-based learning in a compact PCA subspace) is an effective strategy for noisy-feature classification.

Future work may explore more structured noise modeling, metric learning, and stronger robustness techniques (e.g., adversarial or feature-dependent augmentation and calibrated uncertainty estimation) to further improve generalization under unknown test perturbations.
```

```python
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

def noise_stress_test(models, X_val_norm, X_val_pca, y_val, alphas, seed=42):
    """
    Evaluate trained models under increasing Gaussian noise on the validation set.
    Noise is added in the *input space used by each model*:
      - use_pca=True  -> add noise to PCA features
      - use_pca=False -> add noise to standardized 1024-D features
    Returns a DataFrame with mean accuracy over repeats.
    """
    rng = np.random.RandomState(seed)
    records = []

    for alpha in alphas:
        for name, cfg in models.items():
            clf = cfg["model"]
            use_pca = cfg["use_pca"]

            X_base = X_val_pca if use_pca else X_val_norm
            noise = rng.normal(loc=0.0, scale=1.0, size=X_base.shape)
            X_noised = X_base + alpha * noise

            y_pred = clf.predict(X_noised)
            acc = accuracy_score(y_val, y_pred)

            records.append({"alpha": alpha, "method": name, "accuracy": acc})

    return pd.DataFrame(records)


def plot_noise_stress(df, out_path="figures/noise_stress_test_rf.pdf"):
    """
    Plot accuracy vs noise level for each method.
    If you run repeated tests, you can aggregate with mean/std and show error bars.
    """
    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(out_path), exist_ok=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(7.2, 4.2))
    sns.lineplot(data=df, x="alpha", y="accuracy", hue="method", marker="o")
    plt.xlabel(r"Noise strength $\alpha$")
    plt.ylabel("Validation Accuracy")
    plt.title("Noise Stress Test on Validation Set")
    plt.grid(True, linestyle="--", alpha=0.4)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(out_path, dpi=300)
    plt.show()
    print(f"[Figure] Saved noise stress test plot to: {out_path}")
```

## 3) 推荐：加“误差条”（重复多次噪声采样）
单次噪声采样的曲线会有随机波动。更学术的做法是每个 alpha 重复 R 次，画 mean±std。

把 `noise_stress_test` 改成下面这个版本（替换即可）：

```python
def noise_stress_test(models, X_val_norm, X_val_pca, y_val, alphas, seed=42, repeats=5):
    rng = np.random.RandomState(seed)
    records = []

    for alpha in alphas:
        for r in range(repeats):
            for name, cfg in models.items():
                clf = cfg["model"]
                use_pca = cfg["use_pca"]

                X_base = X_val_pca if use_pca else X_val_norm
                noise = rng.normal(0.0, 1.0, size=X_base.shape)
                X_noised = X_base + alpha * noise

                y_pred = clf.predict(X_noised)
                acc = accuracy_score(y_val, y_pred)
                records.append({"alpha": alpha, "method": name, "accuracy": acc, "repeat": r})

    df = pd.DataFrame(records)
    df_agg = df.groupby(["alpha", "method"])["accuracy"].agg(["mean", "std"]).reset_index()
    df_agg.rename(columns={"mean": "acc_mean", "std": "acc_std"}, inplace=True)
    return df_agg
```

```python
import os
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.decomposition import PCA
from sklearn.neighbors import KNeighborsClassifier
from sklearn.discriminant_analysis import LinearDiscriminantAnalysis, QuadraticDiscriminantAnalysis
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier
from sklearn.metrics import accuracy_score


# ==========================================
# 1. Configuration
# ==========================================
CONFIG = {
    "train_feat_path": "train_features.npy",
    "train_label_path": "train_label.npy",
    "test_feat_path": "noised_test_features.npy",   # final test set (no labels)
    "pca_components": 60,                           # PCA dim for KNN/LDA/QDA
    "seed": 42,
    "val_size": 0.2,

    # noise stress test config (on validation set)
    "stress_alphas": [0.0, 0.01, 0.02, 0.05, 0.08, 0.10, 0.12, 0.15],
    "stress_repeats": 5,
    "stress_noise_space": "model_input",  # "model_input" or "original_then_pca"
    # model_input: add noise in the feature space each model uses (PCA for KNN/LDA/QDA, 1024D for RF)
    # original_then_pca: always add noise in 1024D standardized space, then apply PCA for PCA-based models
}


# ==========================================
# 2. Preprocessing helper
# ==========================================
class DataProcessor:
    def __init__(self, pca_components, seed=42):
        self.scaler = StandardScaler()
        self.pca = PCA(n_components=pca_components, random_state=seed)

    def fit_scaler(self, X_train):
        print("   [Preprocessing] Fitting StandardScaler on training split...")
        self.scaler.fit(X_train)

    def transform_scaler(self, X):
        return self.scaler.transform(X)

    def fit_pca(self, X_train_scaled):
        print(f"   [Preprocessing] Fitting PCA (dim={self.pca.n_components_ if hasattr(self.pca,'n_components_') else 'set'})...")
        self.pca.fit(X_train_scaled)

    def transform_pca(self, X_scaled):
        return self.pca.transform(X_scaled)


# ==========================================
# 3. Utility: save submission
# ==========================================
def save_to_csv(predictions, filename):
    df = pd.DataFrame({"ID": np.arange(len(predictions)), "label": predictions})
    df.to_csv(filename, index=False)
    print(f"       Saved predictions to: {filename}")


# ==========================================
# 4. Noise stress test
# ==========================================
def noise_stress_test(
    fitted_models,
    processor: DataProcessor,
    X_val_norm,
    X_val_pca,
    y_val,
    alphas,
    repeats=5,
    seed=42,
    noise_space="model_input"
):
    """
    Evaluate fixed trained models under additive Gaussian noise on validation set.

    noise_space:
      - "model_input": add noise in the space that each model consumes
      - "original_then_pca": add noise in 1024D standardized space first, then PCA-transform for PCA models
    """
    rng = np.random.RandomState(seed)
    records = []

    for alpha in alphas:
        for r in range(repeats):
            # draw noise in 1024-D normalized space (for consistent scaling), used for "original_then_pca"
            noise_1024 = rng.normal(0.0, 1.0, size=X_val_norm.shape)

            for name, cfg in fitted_models.items():
                clf = cfg["model"]
                use_pca = cfg["use_pca"]

                if noise_space == "model_input":
                    X_base = X_val_pca if use_pca else X_val_norm
                    noise = rng.normal(0.0, 1.0, size=X_base.shape)
                    X_noised = X_base + alpha * noise

                elif noise_space == "original_then_pca":
                    # always add noise in 1024D normalized space
                    X_noised_1024 = X_val_norm + alpha * noise_1024
                    if use_pca:
                        X_noised = processor.transform_pca(X_noised_1024)
                    else:
                        X_noised = X_noised_1024
                else:
                    raise ValueError("noise_space must be 'model_input' or 'original_then_pca'")

                y_pred = clf.predict(X_noised)
                acc = accuracy_score(y_val, y_pred)
                records.append({"alpha": alpha, "repeat": r, "method": name, "accuracy": acc})

    df = pd.DataFrame(records)
    df_agg = (
        df.groupby(["alpha", "method"])["accuracy"]
        .agg(["mean", "std"])
        .reset_index()
        .rename(columns={"mean": "acc_mean", "std": "acc_std"})
    )
    return df_agg


def plot_noise_stress(df_agg, out_path="figures/noise_stress_test.pdf"):
    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(out_path), exist_ok=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(7.4, 4.4))
    for method in df_agg["method"].unique():
        sub = df_agg[df_agg["method"] == method].sort_values("alpha")
        plt.errorbar(
            sub["alpha"].values,
            sub["acc_mean"].values,
            yerr=sub["acc_std"].fillna(0.0).values,
            marker="o",
            capsize=3,
            linewidth=1.6,
            label=method,
        )

    plt.xlabel(r"Noise strength $\alpha$")
    plt.ylabel("Validation Accuracy")
    plt.title("Noise Stress Test on Validation Set (mean $\pm$ std)")
    plt.grid(True, linestyle="--", alpha=0.4)
    plt.legend()
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(out_path, dpi=300)
    plt.show()
    print(f"[Figure] Saved to: {out_path}")


# ==========================================
# 5. Main
# ==========================================
def main():
    # --- 1) Load data ---
    print("1. Loading data...")
    if not (os.path.exists(CONFIG["train_feat_path"]) and os.path.exists(CONFIG["train_label_path"]) and os.path.exists(CONFIG["test_feat_path"])):
        raise FileNotFoundError("Missing .npy files. Please place train_features.npy, train_label.npy, noised_test_features.npy in the current directory.")

    X = np.load(CONFIG["train_feat_path"])
    y = np.load(CONFIG["train_label_path"])
    X_test_final = np.load(CONFIG["test_feat_path"])

    print(f"   Train Features: {X.shape}")
    print(f"   Train Labels:   {y.shape}")
    print(f"   Test Features:  {X_test_final.shape}")

    # --- 2) Train/val split (stratified) ---
    print("\n2. Splitting data (stratified hold-out)...")
    X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val = train_test_split(
        X, y,
        test_size=CONFIG["val_size"],
        random_state=CONFIG["seed"],
        stratify=y
    )
    print(f"   Train split: {X_train.shape}, Val split: {X_val.shape}")

    # --- 3) Standardization ---
    print("\n3. Standardization...")
    processor = DataProcessor(pca_components=CONFIG["pca_components"], seed=CONFIG["seed"])
    processor.fit_scaler(X_train)
    X_train_norm = processor.transform_scaler(X_train)
    X_val_norm = processor.transform_scaler(X_val)
    X_test_norm = processor.transform_scaler(X_test_final)

    # --- 4) PCA for KNN/LDA/QDA ---
    print("\n4. PCA for KNN/LDA/QDA...")
    processor.fit_pca(X_train_norm)
    X_train_pca = processor.transform_pca(X_train_norm)
    X_val_pca = processor.transform_pca(X_val_norm)
    X_test_pca = processor.transform_pca(X_test_norm)

    var_ratio = np.sum(processor.pca.explained_variance_ratio_)
    print(f"   PCA explained variance (sum): {var_ratio:.2%}")

    # --- 5) Define models ---
    models = {
        "KNN": {"model": KNeighborsClassifier(n_neighbors=9, weights="distance", n_jobs=-1), "use_pca": True},
        "LDA": {"model": LinearDiscriminantAnalysis(), "use_pca": True},
        "QDA": {"model": QuadraticDiscriminantAnalysis(), "use_pca": True},
        "RandomForest": {"model": RandomForestClassifier(n_estimators=200, max_depth=None, n_jobs=-1, random_state=CONFIG["seed"]), "use_pca": False},
    }

    # --- 6) Train + validate + predict test ---
    print("\n5. Training, validating, and generating submissions...")
    fitted_models = {}

    for name, cfg in models.items():
        print(f"\n   >>> Model: {name} <<<")
        clf = cfg["model"]
        use_pca = cfg["use_pca"]

        if use_pca:
            X_train_in, X_val_in, X_test_in = X_train_pca, X_val_pca, X_test_pca
            print(f"       Input: Preprocess+PCA (dim={CONFIG['pca_components']})")
        else:
            X_train_in, X_val_in, X_test_in = X_train_norm, X_val_norm, X_test_norm
            print("       Input: Preprocess only (dim=1024)")

        clf.fit(X_train_in, y_train)
        fitted_models[name] = {"model": clf, "use_pca": use_pca}

        val_pred = clf.predict(X_val_in)
        acc = accuracy_score(y_val, val_pred)
        print(f"       Validation Accuracy: {acc:.5f}")

        test_pred = clf.predict(X_test_in)
        out_file = f"submission_{name}_pca{CONFIG['pca_components']}.csv" if use_pca else f"submission_{name}.csv"
        save_to_csv(test_pred, out_file)

    # --- 7) Noise stress test plot ---
    print("\n6. Noise stress test on validation set...")
    df_stress = noise_stress_test(
        fitted_models=fitted_models,
        processor=processor,
        X_val_norm=X_val_norm,
        X_val_pca=X_val_pca,
        y_val=y_val,
        alphas=CONFIG["stress_alphas"],
        repeats=CONFIG["stress_repeats"],
        seed=CONFIG["seed"],
        noise_space=CONFIG["stress_noise_space"],
    )

    print("\n   Stress test summary (mean±std):")
    print(df_stress)

    out_fig = "figures/noise_stress_test_rf.pdf"
    plot_noise_stress(df_stress, out_path=out_fig)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

使用说明（简短）：
- 直接运行，会：
  1）训练 KNN/LDA/QDA/RF 并输出各自 submission\_*.csv  
  2）在验证集上做噪声强度压力测试（`CONFIG["stress_alphas"]`），重复 `stress_repeats` 次，输出 `figures/noise_stress_test_rf.pdf` 曲线图（带误差条）
- 若你希望噪声更符合“先在原始特征加噪再PCA”的流程，把：
  ```python
  "stress_noise_space": "original_then_pca"
  ```
  否则默认 `"model_input"`（对各模型输入空间直接加噪）。
图例要在左上方，字体上times new romans且xy label加粗

```python
import os
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.decomposition import PCA
from sklearn.neighbors import KNeighborsClassifier
from sklearn.discriminant_analysis import LinearDiscriminantAnalysis, QuadraticDiscriminantAnalysis
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier
from sklearn.metrics import accuracy_score


# ==========================================
# 1. Configuration
# ==========================================
CONFIG = {
    "train_feat_path": "train_features.npy",
    "train_label_path": "train_label.npy",
    "test_feat_path": "noised_test_features.npy",
    "pca_components": 60,
    "seed": 42,
    "val_size": 0.2,

    # noise stress test config
    "stress_alphas": [0.0, 0.01, 0.02, 0.05, 0.08, 0.10, 0.12, 0.15],
    "stress_repeats": 5,
    "stress_noise_space": "model_input",  # "model_input" or "original_then_pca"

    # figure output
    "stress_fig_path": "figures/noise_stress_test_rf.pdf",
}


# ==========================================
# 2. Preprocessing helper
# ==========================================
class DataProcessor:
    def __init__(self, pca_components, seed=42):
        self.scaler = StandardScaler()
        self.pca = PCA(n_components=pca_components, random_state=seed)

    def fit_scaler(self, X_train):
        print("   [Preprocessing] Fitting StandardScaler on training split...")
        self.scaler.fit(X_train)

    def transform_scaler(self, X):
        return self.scaler.transform(X)

    def fit_pca(self, X_train_scaled):
        print(f"   [Preprocessing] Fitting PCA (dim={self.pca.n_components})...")
        self.pca.fit(X_train_scaled)

    def transform_pca(self, X_scaled):
        return self.pca.transform(X_scaled)


# ==========================================
# 3. Utility: save submission
# ==========================================
def save_to_csv(predictions, filename):
    df = pd.DataFrame({"ID": np.arange(len(predictions)), "label": predictions})
    df.to_csv(filename, index=False)
    print(f"       Saved predictions to: {filename}")


# ==========================================
# 4. Noise stress test
# ==========================================
def noise_stress_test(
    fitted_models,
    processor: DataProcessor,
    X_val_norm,
    X_val_pca,
    y_val,
    alphas,
    repeats=5,
    seed=42,
    noise_space="model_input",
):
    rng = np.random.RandomState(seed)
    records = []

    for alpha in alphas:
        for r in range(repeats):
            noise_1024 = rng.normal(0.0, 1.0, size=X_val_norm.shape)

            for name, cfg in fitted_models.items():
                clf = cfg["model"]
                use_pca = cfg["use_pca"]

                if noise_space == "model_input":
                    X_base = X_val_pca if use_pca else X_val_norm
                    noise = rng.normal(0.0, 1.0, size=X_base.shape)
                    X_noised = X_base + alpha * noise

                elif noise_space == "original_then_pca":
                    X_noised_1024 = X_val_norm + alpha * noise_1024
                    X_noised = processor.transform_pca(X_noised_1024) if use_pca else X_noised_1024
                else:
                    raise ValueError("noise_space must be 'model_input' or 'original_then_pca'")

                acc = accuracy_score(y_val, clf.predict(X_noised))
                records.append({"alpha": alpha, "repeat": r, "method": name, "accuracy": acc})

    df = pd.DataFrame(records)
    df_agg = (
        df.groupby(["alpha", "method"])["accuracy"]
        .agg(["mean", "std"])
        .reset_index()
        .rename(columns={"mean": "acc_mean", "std": "acc_std"})
    )
    return df_agg


def plot_noise_stress(df_agg, out_path):
    # --- Font/style settings: Times New Roman + bold axis labels ---
    plt.rcParams.update({
        "font.family": "Times New Roman",
        "mathtext.fontset": "stix",   # math symbols closer to Times
        "font.size": 12,
        "axes.titlesize": 12,
        "axes.labelsize": 13,
        "legend.fontsize": 11,
        "xtick.labelsize": 11,
        "ytick.labelsize": 11,
        "pdf.fonttype": 42,
        "ps.fonttype": 42,
    })

    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(out_path), exist_ok=True)

    plt.figure(figsize=(7.4, 4.4))
    for method in df_agg["method"].unique():
        sub = df_agg[df_agg["method"] == method].sort_values("alpha")
        plt.errorbar(
            sub["alpha"].values,
            sub["acc_mean"].values,
            yerr=sub["acc_std"].fillna(0.0).values,
            marker="o",
            capsize=3,
            linewidth=1.6,
            label=method,
        )

    plt.xlabel(r"Noise strength $\alpha$", fontweight="bold")
    plt.ylabel("Validation Accuracy", fontweight="bold")
    plt.title("Noise Stress Test on Validation Set (mean ± std)")

    plt.grid(True, linestyle="--", alpha=0.35)

    # Legend in upper-left
    plt.legend(loc="upper left", frameon=True)

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(out_path, dpi=300)
    plt.show()
    print(f"[Figure] Saved to: {out_path}")


# ==========================================
# 5. Main
# ==========================================
def main():
    print("1. Loading data...")
    if not (os.path.exists(CONFIG["train_feat_path"]) and
            os.path.exists(CONFIG["train_label_path"]) and
            os.path.exists(CONFIG["test_feat_path"])):
        raise FileNotFoundError(
            "Missing .npy files. Please place train_features.npy, train_label.npy, noised_test_features.npy in the current directory."
        )

    X = np.load(CONFIG["train_feat_path"])
    y = np.load(CONFIG["train_label_path"])
    X_test_final = np.load(CONFIG["test_feat_path"])

    print(f"   Train Features: {X.shape}")
    print(f"   Train Labels:   {y.shape}")
    print(f"   Test Features:  {X_test_final.shape}")

    print("\n2. Splitting data (stratified hold-out)...")
    X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val = train_test_split(
        X, y,
        test_size=CONFIG["val_size"],
        random_state=CONFIG["seed"],
        stratify=y
    )
    print(f"   Train split: {X_train.shape}, Val split: {X_val.shape}")

    print("\n3. Standardization...")
    processor = DataProcessor(pca_components=CONFIG["pca_components"], seed=CONFIG["seed"])
    processor.fit_scaler(X_train)
    X_train_norm = processor.transform_scaler(X_train)
    X_val_norm = processor.transform_scaler(X_val)
    X_test_norm = processor.transform_scaler(X_test_final)

    print("\n4. PCA for KNN/LDA/QDA...")
    processor.fit_pca(X_train_norm)
    X_train_pca = processor.transform_pca(X_train_norm)
    X_val_pca = processor.transform_pca(X_val_norm)
    X_test_pca = processor.transform_pca(X_test_norm)

    print(f"   PCA explained variance (sum): {np.sum(processor.pca.explained_variance_ratio_):.2%}")

    models = {
        "KNN": {"model": KNeighborsClassifier(n_neighbors=9, weights="distance", n_jobs=-1), "use_pca": True},
        "LDA": {"model": LinearDiscriminantAnalysis(), "use_pca": True},
        "QDA": {"model": QuadraticDiscriminantAnalysis(), "use_pca": True},
        "RandomForest": {"model": RandomForestClassifier(n_estimators=200, max_depth=None, n_jobs=-1, random_state=CONFIG["seed"]), "use_pca": False},
    }

    print("\n5. Training, validating, and generating submissions...")
    fitted_models = {}

    for name, cfg in models.items():
        print(f"\n   >>> Model: {name} <<<")
        clf = cfg["model"]
        use_pca = cfg["use_pca"]

        if use_pca:
            X_train_in, X_val_in, X_test_in = X_train_pca, X_val_pca, X_test_pca
            print(f"       Input: Preprocess+PCA (dim={CONFIG['pca_components']})")
        else:
            X_train_in, X_val_in, X_test_in = X_train_norm, X_val_norm, X_test_norm
            print("       Input: Preprocess only (dim=1024)")

        clf.fit(X_train_in, y_train)
        fitted_models[name] = {"model": clf, "use_pca": use_pca}

        val_acc = accuracy_score(y_val, clf.predict(X_val_in))
        print(f"       Validation Accuracy: {val_acc:.5f}")

        test_pred = clf.predict(X_test_in)
        out_file = f"submission_{name}_pca{CONFIG['pca_components']}.csv" if use_pca else f"submission_{name}.csv"
        save_to_csv(test_pred, out_file)

    print("\n6. Noise stress test on validation set...")
    df_stress = noise_stress_test(
        fitted_models=fitted_models,
        processor=processor,
        X_val_norm=X_val_norm,
        X_val_pca=X_val_pca,
        y_val=y_val,
        alphas=CONFIG["stress_alphas"],
        repeats=CONFIG["stress_repeats"],
        seed=CONFIG["seed"],
        noise_space=CONFIG["stress_noise_space"],
    )

    print("\n   Stress test summary (mean±std):")
    print(df_stress)

    plot_noise_stress(df_stress, out_path=CONFIG["stress_fig_path"])


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

说明：
- 图例固定在左上角：`plt.legend(loc="upper left", ...)`
- Times New Roman：通过 `plt.rcParams.update({"font.family": "Times New Roman", ...})`
- x/y label 加粗：`fontweight="bold"`
- 若你的环境没有 Times New Roman，会自动fallback；如需强制指定字体文件路径，我也可以给你一版更稳的写法（matplotlib.font_manager）。
